title: 10 best herbal creams for one side headache

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory compound, anethole, helps reduce swelling and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to one-sided headaches.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic compounds present in fennel cream, such as borneol and borneol acetate, help relax tense muscles and alleviate spasms that can cause one-sided headaches.
Pain-relieving property: Fennel's essential oil contains a compound called fenchone, which has natural analgesic and anti-pain properties, helping to reduce pain associated with one-sided headaches.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with one side headache because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compound anethole in fennel oil has been shown to relax tense muscles and improve blood circulation, which can help alleviate pressure points that cause one-sided headaches. Additionally, the cooling and soothing effects of fennel creams can provide quick relief from pain and discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for this common condition. This can provide significant relief for individuals experiencing migraines or tension headaches.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: This sesquiterpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate one-sided headaches by reducing pain and inflammation in the affected area.

Anethole: A phenolic compound that has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the muscles and nervous system, anethole can help relieve tension and alleviate one-sided headaches caused by stress or muscle strain.

Furanocoumarins: These compounds have been found to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce muscle spasms and inflammation that may contribute to one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their essential oil content, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: Used for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate headache symptoms.
Flowers: Used due to their volatile oil content, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Infuse the dried foeniculum vulgare in 500ml of organic vegetable oil at 45 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Combine the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Blend the mixture until it reaches a smooth and consistent texture then pour into a glass jar.

remedy_properties: Cools and soothes: Peppermint's natural cooling effect helps to reduce inflammation and ease tension in the affected area, providing rapid relief from headache discomfort.
Eases pain: Menthol, a key active compound in peppermint, has analgesic properties that help numb the pain associated with one-sided headaches, making them feel more manageable.
Relaxes muscles: The relaxing and calming effects of peppermint can help to ease muscle tension, which is often a contributing factor to one-sided headaches, promoting a sense of relief and comfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with one side headache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint creams can help to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, which can ease the pain associated with one side headaches. Additionally, peppermint's cooling effect can help to numb the pain and reduce inflammation, providing quick relief from one side headaches. This makes peppermint creams a popular natural remedy for this common condition.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps relieve one side headaches by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory, reducing pain and discomfort in the affected area.
Limonene: Limonene, a terpene found in peppermint, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with one side headaches.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound in peppermint, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate one side headaches by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most widely used part of Mentha x piperita due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are used for their ability to reduce pain and inflammation, particularly in the case of one-sided headaches.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used due to their high content of menthol and menthone, which are released when they are crushed, providing relief from headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves from a pesticide-free garden at dawn when dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested leaves in a 10-liter still at 100°c for 2 hours to obtain essential oil.
Mix 1 teaspoon of glycerin with 10 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof glass bowl and melt them in a double boiler.
Combine 1 teaspoon of mentha x piperita essential oil with the melted glycerin and beeswax mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a 100-gram container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal sage creams contain compounds like rosmarinic acid and camphor, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area.
Pain-relieving properties: Sage contains salvinorin A, a natural analgesic that can help alleviate headache pain by blocking pain receptors and reducing inflammation in the nerves.

Antispasmodic properties: The essential oils present in sage creams, such as camphor and borneol, have antispasmodic effects, which can help relax tense muscles and blood vessels, alleviating migraines and one-sided headaches.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with one side headache because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in sage, including rosmarinic acid and camphor, work to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. By applying sage creams to the affected side of the head, individuals may experience relief from one-sided headaches. The creams can also help to relax tense muscles, improve blood flow, and reduce tension, leading to a reduction in headache symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with one-sided headaches.

Carnosic acid: This terpene has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area, providing relief from one-sided headaches.

Salvianolic acid: This flavonoid has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with one-sided headaches, while also promoting blood flow and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain active compounds like salvene and carnosic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: They possess flavonoids and phenolic acids that contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: They contain a high concentration of salvene and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of salvia officinalis leaves and flowers when in full bloom at dawn.
Steep the harvested salvia officinalis in 200 milliliters of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infusion to remove the solids and discard the plant material immediately.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 100-110 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine the cooled salvia infusion with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and stir until smooth.

remedy_properties: Sedative and Calming Properties: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for one-sided headaches. By promoting relaxation, it can ease tension in the neck and scalp muscles, reducing pain and discomfort.

Anti-Inflammatory and Analgesic Properties: Lavender contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with one-sided headaches. These compounds may also help alleviate muscle spasms and tension.

Antioxidant and Antimicrobial Properties: Lavender's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, while its antimicrobial properties can help prevent infections that may contribute to headache development. This can be especially beneficial for people who experience frequent or recurring one-sided headaches.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with one-sided headaches because of its calming and soothing properties. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lavender oil can help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area, providing relief from tension and stress. Additionally, the sedative effects of lavender can promote relaxation, which can help alleviate migraine and tension headache symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce pain, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution to one-sided headaches.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a natural anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce stress and alleviate headache symptoms.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve pain and reduce inflammation associated with one-sided headaches.
Camphor: Possesses analgesic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to ease muscle tension and alleviate pain in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are used in creams for one-sided headaches due to their calming and soothing properties, which help to relieve tension and ease pain.
Leaves: Lavender leaves are used in creams for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with one-sided headaches.

Stems: Lavender stems contain essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with one-sided headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has dried completely.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried flowers or 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the cooled liquid with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 5 to 10 minutes or until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Rosemary's antispasmodic compounds help to relax and ease muscle contractions, which can contribute to one-sided headaches by reducing tension in the neck and scalp.

Additionally, rosemary's ability to stimulate the mind and promote mental clarity can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related headaches. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers on a sunny day.
Infuse 1 cup of carrier oil with 1/4 cup of rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter with 1/4 cup of infused oil.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and store in a cool dry place for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm cream helps to relax muscle spasms and reduce tension, which can contribute to one-sided headaches such as migraines or temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate one-sided headaches such as tension headaches.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves when in full bloom for maximum potency and fragrance.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 40c for 2 hours to preserve the essential oils.
Infuse 50g of dried melissa leaves in 500ml of base oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container to separate solids from liquid.
Mix the infused oil with 20% beeswax and 10% vitamin e oil to create a stable cream base.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, thereby alleviating headache symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, which may be contributing factors to one-sided headaches.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with one-sided headache because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These pigments may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to headaches, including one-sided ones.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used because of their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin and muscles of the head and neck.

remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers in their peak bloom and dry them for 1-2 weeks in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit.
Steep 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of distilled water in a double boiler at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools to a consistency suitable for application to the skin.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Helps to reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, which can contribute to headache symptoms.

Hypericin: Has anti-platelet and blood thinning properties, which can help to prevent blood clots and improve blood flow to the brain, potentially alleviating one-sided headaches caused by reduced blood flow.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with one side headache because of its potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which may help alleviate the pain associated with one-sided headaches.
Hypericin: It has antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, potentially contributing to the relief of one-sided headache symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100g of fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves in the summer months when the plant is in bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Create a liquid extract by infusing 20g of dried st john's wort in 100ml of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks.
Mix the liquid extract with 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter to create a smooth cream.
Fill small containers with the herbal cream and label them for storage and later use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains compounds like eucalyptol and alpha-pinene that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, providing relief from one-sided headaches.

Pain-relieving properties: The cream's active ingredients, such as cineole and bornyl acetate, have analgesic properties, which can help numb the pain associated with a one-sided headache, allowing for quicker recovery.
Antispasmodic properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's ability to relax muscles and reduce spasms can help alleviate tension headaches, including those that cause unilateral pain (one-sided).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with one-sided headache because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cooling effect of Eucalyptus globulus oil can help to ease tension and reduce pain in the affected area. The menthol content in Eucalyptus globulus creams can also help to relax muscles and improve blood circulation, thereby reducing the severity of one-sided headaches. This natural remedy can provide quick and effective relief from the pain and discomfort associated with one-sided headaches.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol helps with one-sided headache by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation in the affected area.
Cineole: Cineole, also known as eucalyptol, has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate tension headaches and reduce the frequency and severity of one-sided headaches.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high eucalyptol content, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve headache pain.
Barks: They are used because of their high tannin content, which has astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate headache symptoms.
Stems: They are used as a source of eucalyptol, which has been shown to have a positive effect on blood vessels and can help relieve one-sided headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh leaves of eucalyptus globulus on a dry sunny day to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve their properties.
Mix the dried leaves with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl at 10-second intervals to create a smooth consistency.
Add a few drops of vitamin e oil and a teaspoon of essential oil of eucalyptus globulus to the melted mixture and stir well to combine.

remedy_properties: Methyl Salicylate: Relieves pain and reduces inflammation by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and fever. This property makes it effective in easing headache symptoms.
Gaultheria: Contains analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to numb the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort associated with one-side headaches.

Vasodilating Properties: Wintergreen's active compound, methyl salicylate, also has a vasodilating effect, which helps to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow to the affected area, further relieving headache symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with one side headache because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's main active compound, methyl salicylate, is a natural pain reliever that helps to reduce inflammation and ease tension in the affected area. By applying the cream directly to the affected side of the head, individuals can experience relief from one-sided headaches caused by stress, tension, or migraines, allowing for a sense of calm and relaxation to return.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: This glycoside compound is a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent that helps alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins and other pain-causing chemicals in the body.

Methyl salicylate: A volatile oil derived from wintergreen, methyl salicylate is a potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area, providing relief from one-sided headaches.

Gaultherin: A polyphenolic compound found in Gaultheria procumbens, gaultherin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high content of salicylic acid, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain.
Roots: The roots are used for their bioactive compounds, particularly salicin and glycosides, which contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are utilized for their content of essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in late spring or early summer when the plant is in bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for at least two weeks.
Infuse 1 part dried gaultheria procumbens in 2 parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax to 8 ounces of the infused oil and heat the mixture gently until the wax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with one side headache because it contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds that target the source of pain. The cream's active ingredients, such as glycosides and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and ease tension in the affected area, providing relief from pressure and discomfort. This localized approach allows the cream to target the specific spot causing the headache, bringing fast and effective relief without overwhelming the entire body with medication.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and anthocyanins, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, providing relief from headache symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich ingredients in elder cream help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, contributing to headache development and severity.

Vasodilatory effects: Elder cream's vasodilatory compounds, such as flavonoids, help relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can alleviate tension headaches by reducing blood vessel constriction and promoting healthy blood flow.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease tension in the affected side of the head, providing relief from headache pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the elder plant contain compounds that act as natural pain relievers, helping to alleviate one-sided headache symptoms by reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves for the desired quantity of 20 grams.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7 days.
Grind the dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered sambucus nigra with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at medium heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with one side headache because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that target the root cause of unilateral headaches. The anti-inflammatory properties of dandelion soothe and calm inflamed blood vessels, while its analgesic properties relieve pain and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion's ability to reduce stress and anxiety levels helps to mitigate trigger factors that can contribute to one-sided head pain. Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help protect the body from free radical damage, which can contribute to oxidative stress and headache development.

Vasodilatory effects: Dandelion's bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes, can cause blood vessels to dilate, which may help increase blood flow to the affected area, potentially reducing tension and alleviating headache pain.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Combine the ingredients in a saucepan with 1 cup of olive oil and heat over medium flame.
Steep for 30 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the infused oil and stir until melted.
Pour the cooled cream into small tin containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with one side headache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as chamazulene and sesquiterpene lactones, work to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. Additionally, yarrow's ability to improve blood circulation may help to relieve tension and pressure that can cause headaches. By applying the cream topically to the affected side of the head, individuals may experience a rapid reduction in headache severity and frequency.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the blood vessels, potentially alleviating one-sided headache symptoms.

Vasoconstricting properties: Yarrow's active compounds, such as chamazulene, have a vasoconstrictive effect, which means they can help narrow blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the affected area, thereby reducing migraine-like pain.

Antispasmodic effects: Yarrow's essential oils, including estragole and anethole, have antispasmodic properties, which can help relax muscles and relieve tension in the neck and head, potentially alleviating one-sided headache symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, which help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with one-sided headaches.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in soothing headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 20g of dried achillea millefolium leaves with 100ml of cold-pressed extra virgin olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dry place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to allow infusion to occur.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids after 1 hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with one side headache because of its unique ability to relax and reduce inflammation in the affected areas. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, which can contribute to the throbbing pain associated with migraines and tension headaches. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can help to ease muscle tension and pressure points on the affected side of the head, providing fast and effective relief from one-sided headaches.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle creams contain compounds like oleic acid and linoleic acid that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area.

Pain-relieving properties: Nettle creams also contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and antinociceptive (pain-relieving) activities, helping to alleviate headache pain.

Vasodilatory effects: The beta-carotene and other antioxidants present in nettle creams can help dilate blood vessels, which may improve blood flow and reduce constriction of the temporal artery, a common cause of one-side headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, providing relief from a one-side headache.
Barks: The barks of the stinging nettle plant have been traditionally used to treat various types of headaches, including one-sided headaches, due to their analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh nettle leaves from organic sources or dried ones rehydrated in water.
Steep the harvested nettles in 200ml of boiling water for 30 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and discard the solids before adding 50g of beeswax and 20ml of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat stirring occasionally until the beeswax and coconut oil melt and combine with the infusion.
Remove from heat and allow to cool before pouring into a sterilized container for storage and later use as a topical cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with one side headache because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active ingredients in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, work to reduce swelling and ease tension in the affected area. This targeted relief helps to alleviate pressure on the trigeminal nerve, which is often responsible for one-sided headaches. Additionally, lemongrass's calming effects help to relax the scalp and forehead muscles, further reducing pain and discomfort. Overall, lemongrass creams provide a natural and effective way to address one-sided headaches without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the blood vessels on the affected side of the head.

Antispasmodic properties: The essential oils present in lemongrass, such as citronellal and limonene, can help relax muscle spasms and relieve tension in the neck and scalp, which may be contributing to the one-sided headache.

Analgesic properties: Lemongrass contains a compound called myrcene, which has analgesic and pain-relieving effects, helping to alleviate the throbbing or pulsating sensation associated with one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help reduce pain and inflammation associated with one-sided headaches.
Rhyzomes: Containing analgesic and antispasmodic properties, rhyzomes alleviate tension and relieve muscle contractions that cause one-sided headaches.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and grind into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered cymbopogon citratus leaves with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 10 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated, then remove from heat.
Pour the cooled mixture into glass jars and store in refrigerator for at least 2 hours before use, allowing it to solidify completely.
Apply a thin layer of the cymbopogon citratus cream directly to the affected side of the forehead as needed throughout the day to alleviate headache symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Basil creams helps with one side headache because of its unique ability to target the specific areas of the brain responsible for pain processing. The cream's active ingredients, including eugenol and linalool, are absorbed through the skin and work together to reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels, which can help alleviate tension headaches that often occur on one side of the head. By directly addressing the root cause of the headache, basil creams provide targeted relief for a rapid and effective recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Basil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as eugenol and linalool, help to reduce swelling and pain in blood vessels, which may contribute to one-sided headaches.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in basil, like estragole and citronellal, can help to relax muscles and vasodilate blood vessels, alleviating tension and reducing the frequency of one-sided headaches.

Antioxidant properties: Basil's antioxidant compounds, such as beta-carotene and vitamin C, help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which may contribute to the development of one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for soothing one-sided headaches.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, barks have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling caused by one-sided headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh ocimum basilicum leaves and dry them using shade drying method for 7 days.
Grind the dried basil leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle and store it in air-tight container.
Mix 20g of powdered basil with 100g of cold-pressed coconut oil and 50g of beeswax pellets in double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Fill sterile glass jars with the prepared cream and label them, allowing it to cool and solidify before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marijuana creams helps with one side headache because they target the affected area directly, providing fast and effective relief from pain and discomfort. The active ingredients in these creams, such as cannabidiol (CBD) and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), bind to receptors in the skin and brain, reducing inflammation and constricting blood vessels that may be contributing to the headache. As a result, herbal marijuana creams can quickly alleviate pressure and tension on the affected side of the head, providing instant relief from one-sided headaches.
remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): Helps to reduce inflammation, which is often a contributing factor to migraines and tension headaches, by interacting with the body's endocannabinoid system.
Terpenes: Provide a calming and relaxing effect on the mind and body, helping to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate headache symptoms.

Cannabis-activated compounds (e.g., THC): Help to relax muscles, reduce pain, and improve sleep quality, all of which are beneficial for alleviating headache symptoms such as tension, pressure, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in cannabigerol, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling associated with a one-sided headache.
Barks: The barks contain a high concentration of salicylic acid, similar to aspirin, which can help relieve the throbbing pain and discomfort caused by a one-sided headache.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cannabis flowers with a minimum of 15% thc content and dry them thoroughly for at least three days.
Grind 20 grams of dried cannabis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground cannabis with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a double boiler over low heat for four hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container, pressing on solids to extract as much oil as possible.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the extracted oil mixture and stir until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with one side headache because of its ability to balance hormones in the body, particularly progesterone. When progesterone levels become imbalanced, it can lead to migraines and other types of headaches that affect only one side of the head. The chaste tree herb has a natural ability to regulate hormonal fluctuations, which can help alleviate the symptoms of one-sided headaches. Regular use of these creams may provide relief from frequent or recurring episodes of one-side headaches.
remedy_properties: Spasmolytic activity: Chasteberry cream's spasmolytic activity helps to relax and reduce muscle contractions in the head and neck, which can contribute to one-sided headaches.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in chasteberry creams may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the blood vessels and nerves of the affected side, alleviating headache symptoms.

Vasodilatory effects: Chasteberry cream's vasodilatory properties can help widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the affected area, which may help relieve one-sided headaches caused by vasoconstriction or poor circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chaste tree contain sesquiterpenes and iridoid glycosides, which help to relax muscles and reduce inflammation, making them effective in alleviating one-sided headaches.

Flowers: The flowers of the chaste tree are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm the affected area and relieve headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried vitex agnus-castus flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Melt 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat over medium-low heat for approximately 30-40 minutes.
Add the ground vitex agnus-castus powder to the melted coconut oil mixture and stir well with a spoon until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for about 1-2 hours or overnight in the refrigerator.
Transfer the cream into an airtight container and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or use immediately.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for one side headache are topical ointments that utilize plant extracts to alleviate pain and discomfort on one side of the head. These creams help by providing localized relief, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal creams for one side headache include Foeniculum vulgare, Zingiber officinale, Mentha x piperita, Achillea millefolium, Eucalyptus globulus, Lavandula angustifolia, Melaleuca alternifolia, Cinnamomum verum, Boswellia serrata, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, one side headaches can severely impact daily life, causing fatigue, mood swings, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting work, relationships, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to one-side headache that are treated with herbal creams are migraines, neuralgia, and trigeminal nerve pain. Herbal creams containing ingredients like capsaicin, peppermint oil, and wintergreen oil can help alleviate these conditions by reducing inflammation, soothing the nerves, and relieving pain. These topical treatments can provide quick and effective relief without the need for prescription medication or invasive procedures.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with one side headache is a blend of peppermint and eucalyptus. Peppermint helps relax the tense muscles and improves blood flow, while eucalyptus oil reduces inflammation and eases sinus pressure. Adding a small amount of lavender oil can also help with relaxation and pain relief. Apply these creams together in a gentle circular motion to the affected area, 2-3 times a day, to experience relief from one side headaches. This combination can provide quick and effective pain relief.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, Italian folk remedies to treat headache were documented in a vast corpus of literature sources. The study involved a detailed search of scientific databases such as Medline and Scopus to uncover recent results concerning the anti-inflammatory, anti-nociceptive, and analgesic activities of plant-based remedies used in Italian folk medicine. The results showed that 58 (78.4%) plant-based remedies demonstrated a range of anti-inflammatory, anti-nociceptive, and analgesic activities, making herbal creams a good option for treating one-sided headaches as they can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams helps with one side headache because they contain an essential oil called anethole, which has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. This oil works by reducing swelling and relaxing tense muscles in the neck and scalp, which can contribute to headaches. By applying Foeniculum vulgare creams to the affected area, individuals may experience relief from pain and discomfort. The creams can also help to improve blood circulation and promote relaxation, further alleviating headache symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Acts as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relieve pain associated with one-sided headaches.
Anethole: Exhibits a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm and reduce the frequency and intensity of one-sided headaches.
Phenolic compounds (e.g., caffeic acid): Possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their anise oil content, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate headache pain.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to release essential oils, which contain compounds that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area.
Stems: They are used due to their content of fennel oil, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help relieve headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems from mature plants after 2-3 years of growth.
Infuse 20g of dried foeniculum vulgare in 100ml of oil at 70-80c for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids.
Combine 50g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter and 50ml of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture at room temperature for 10-15 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with one side headache because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which target the underlying cause of the pain. The cream's active ingredient, gingerols and shogaols, work to reduce inflammation in the affected area, providing relief from the throbbing sensation. Additionally, the warming sensation produced by the cream helps to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, further alleviating the pain and discomfort associated with one-sided headaches. This natural remedy offers a soothing and effective solution.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help with one-sided headaches by reducing inflammation and pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Shogaols: Contribute to alleviating one-sided headaches by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals and reducing inflammation in the affected area.

Gingerols' isomers, (6)-Gingerdiol and (8)-Gingerdiol: Help with one-sided headaches by displaying potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them potential candidates for pain management.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate headache pain.
Leaves: Although not as commonly used as the rhyzomes, ginger leaves may be used in some herbal creams due to their potential ability to reduce inflammation and ease pain.
Buds: Ginger buds, similar to the rhyzomes, may be used in creams due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that can help to soothe and reduce headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of grated zingiber officinale root in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Steep the infused mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain it to obtain the liquid extract.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler for 5 minutes.
Add the herbal extract and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel to the beeswax mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with one-sided headaches because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The peppermint oil in these creams contains compounds like menthol and menthone, which help to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow to the affected area. This can reduce pain and discomfort associated with migraines and other types of one-sided headaches. Additionally, mentha x piperita's ability to reduce inflammation can also help to alleviate pressure and tension that may be contributing to the headache.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with one-sided headaches by numbing the nerves and reducing pain perception, providing quick relief from pain and discomfort.
Eugenol: Eugenol acts as a potent anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and swelling in the affected area, which helps alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with one-sided headaches.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area, providing relief from one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to relieve headache symptoms.
Stems: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce headache pain and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: They are used for their high content of menthol and other active compounds, which help to relax muscles and reduce headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in 2 liters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 50 grams of candelilla wax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine the infused liquid with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in the double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and becomes a smooth cream that sets at room temperature in about 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams helps with one side headache because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the affected area. The herb's analgesic properties also help to reduce pain and discomfort, providing relief from the headache. Additionally, its vasodilatory properties help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the affected area and promoting healing. remedy_constituents: Achillol: Helps with one-sided headache by acting as a vasodilator, which can help relax the blood vessels and reduce pressure on the nerves.
Apigenin: Helps with one-sided headache by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agent, which can help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles.
Rosmarinic acid: Helps with one-sided headache by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties which can help relieve headache symptoms.
Flowers: They are used for their analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate one-sided headache pain.
Roots: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the morning when they are at peak potency.
Steep the harvested herbs in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl to remove solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of coconut oil or shea butter with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler to create a smooth base.
Combine the infused liquid with the melted base mixture and whip until it thickens into a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with one-sided headache because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area. The menthol content in these creams acts as a natural analgesic, providing quick relief from headache symptoms. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus has a decongestant effect, which helps to clear nasal passages and sinuses, often associated with migraines and one-sided headaches. This holistic approach addresses both the physical and respiratory aspects of the condition.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (eucalyptol): This compound helps by acting as a natural decongestant, reducing inflammation and congestion in the sinuses and nasal passages, which can be a contributing factor to one-sided headaches.
Dihydrocoumarin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with one-sided headaches.

Caffeic acid: This compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels, potentially contributing to the alleviation of one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used to make creams for one-sided headaches due to their high eucalyptol content, which provides a cooling and decongestant effect.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with one-sided headaches.
Oils extracted from Leaves: The eucalyptol in Eucalyptus globulus oils extracted from leaves is used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in one-sided headaches.
remedy_recipe: Mix 250ml of distilled water with 20g of eucalyptus globulus dried leaves in a heat-resistant bowl.
Steep the mixture at 100°c for 15 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth to obtain the herbal extract.
Combine 100g of beeswax, 50g of shea butter, and 20g of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Gradually add the herbal extract to the melted wax mixture while stirring constantly to achieve a smooth texture.
Pour the cooled mixture into a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with one side headache because its active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have potent anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties. These compounds work to relax tense muscles, improve blood circulation, and reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. As a result, the cream can provide relief from one-sided headaches by addressing the underlying causes of the pain, promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system to alleviate the discomfort associated with this type of headache.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps to reduce pain and inflammation, which can contribute to one-sided headaches.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a sedative and calming effect, which can help alleviate the underlying stress and anxiety that may be exacerbating one-sided headaches.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in blood vessels that may be causing one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce headache pain and promote relaxation.
Flowers: Employed for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties to alleviate pain and reduce headache severity.
Seeds: Utilized for their sedative and anti-inflammatory effects to calm the mind and body, relieving headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of hot water for 10 minutes to create herbal extract.
Combine 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Add 2 tablespoons of the herbal extract to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Whip 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of honey until smooth and creamy in texture.
Blend the herbal mixture with the beeswax mixture until a smooth and consistent cream forms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with one side headache because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream, often referred to as tea tree oil, contains compounds that reduce swelling and ease pain associated with migraines and tension headaches. When applied topically, it may help to relax tense muscles and calm the nervous system, providing relief from one-sided headaches. The natural ingredients in Melaleuca alternifolia cream work to soothe and calm the affected area, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and swelling in the affected area of the head.
Cymene: Exhibits antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relax tense muscles and reduce inflammation that may be contributing to one-sided headaches.
Linalool: Demonstrates sedative and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce inflammation that may be causing pain on one side of the head.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help alleviate headache symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves also contain other essential oils such as cineole, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches.
Leaves: The leaves' essential oils can also help relax muscles, improve circulation, and reduce stress, all of which can contribute to relief from one-sided headaches.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250ml of oil from melaleuca alternifolia leaves using a cold press method for 2 hours.
Mix the extracted oil with 100g of beeswax in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 50g of distilled water and 10g of a natural emulsifier to the mixture and stir for 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 70-80 degrees celsius for 15 minutes to facilitate blending and thickening.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and let it cool and set for 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams helps with one side headache because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in Cinnamomum verum, such as cinnamaldehyde, possess potent pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to ease headache pain. The topical application of these creams directly targets the affected area, providing quick relief from one-sided headache pain. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum creams can help relax tense muscles and improve blood circulation, further contributing to their headache-relieving properties.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This constituent has a potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with one-sided headaches.
Cinnamic acid: Cinnamic acid has a vasodilatory effect, which may help to increase blood flow and reduce tension in blood vessels, potentially alleviating one-sided headache symptoms.
Eugenol: Eugenol has a neuroprotective effect and may help to reduce inflammation and pain in the nervous system, which can contribute to one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams for one side headache due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to relieve pain and reduce inflammation.
Barks: The bark of Cinnamomum verum is also used, as it contains compounds with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate headaches.

Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of Cinnamomum verum may also be employed in the production of creams for one side headache, likely due to their potential anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried cinnamomum verum bark from mature trees in a clean environment for 2 hours.
Infuse the dried bark in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to extract its properties.
Strain the mixture and combine 1 cup of beeswax with the infused liquid in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax is fully dissolved in 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata creams helps with one-sided headache because they contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that target the root cause of the pain. The active compound boswellic acid reduces inflammation in the nerves and blood vessels, alleviating pressure and discomfort. By soothing the affected area, Boswellia serrata creams provide quick relief from pain and discomfort, allowing individuals to resume their daily activities without the burden of a headache. Regular application can also help in preventing future occurrences.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): It helps reduce inflammation in the nerves, which may contribute to one-sided headaches by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Terpenoids (specifically, boswellic acids): They help reduce inflammation and pain by blocking the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and 5-LOX, which may be involved in the development of one-sided headaches.
α-boswellic acid: It may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, contributing to the relief of one-sided headaches by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headache pain.
Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata have been traditionally used to make creams for pain relief, as they contain boswellic acids that can reduce inflammation and alleviate headache pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata contain boswellic acids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for making creams that treat one side headache.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of base cream consisting of 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler at a temperature of 180-190 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine 50 grams of dried boswellia serrata powder with 100 ml of jojoba oil in a small bowl.
Add the boswellia serrata powder mixture to the melted cream and stir until well combined.
Pour the cream into small jars and let cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with one side headache because they promote improved blood flow to the brain. The active compounds in ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to relax and dilate blood vessels. This increased blood flow can help to alleviate tension and pressure on one side of the head, thereby providing relief from headache symptoms. Regular use of ginkgo biloba creams may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of one-sided headaches.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps with one side headache by improving blood flow to the brain and reducing inflammation, which can alleviate pain and discomfort caused by reduced oxygenation.
Quercetin: Contributes to headache relief by its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling in the affected area.
Flavonoids: Aids in headache relief by exerting a neuroprotective effect, which can help to stabilize and repair damaged brain tissue and reduce the severity of headache symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, potentially relieving headache symptoms.
Seeds: They contain ginkgolides and bilobalide, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with one-sided headaches.
Leaves: They also contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, potentially alleviating headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall season when they are fully ripe and dry them thoroughly for 2 weeks.
Extract the active compounds from dried ginkgo biloba leaves using a solvent such as glycerin or jojoba oil in a 1:2 ratio.
Mix the extracted ginkgo biloba extract with a carrier oil such as coconut oil or shea butter in a 1:10 ratio.
Blend the mixture with a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax to achieve a smooth and stable consistency.
Pour the final mixture into a sterilized container and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours.
